notes: Too bad the resolution must be set by editing a file, as others have already mentioned, because otherwise this would be a solid Platinum. 12 hours in, not a single crash or issue. Performance is good.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: Can't set resolution ingame - must do it in config file. Changing other graphics settings while level is loaded slows game to a crawl, sometimes doesn't launch, performance noticeably worse than in Windows.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes:
The game launches sometimes. Usually though the game opens in to the loading screen and then after a few seconds it crashes.
Sometimes i get a "Installed video device does not support DirectX 11 features".

When the game does launch, the resolution isn't detected in-game and is run at the default resolution unless modified in the render.cfg file. I haven't had the chance to try the game after modifying the config file, since the game won't launch
Reverting config changes back to original values doesn't resolve the issue.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: First, I needed to upgrade to proton 3.16-6 for the game to even launch.  Then the resolution cannot be changed in-game, and I had to edit the render.cfg file manually.  Finally, audio didn't work out of the box.  Others had the exact same problem at https://steamcommunity.com/app/356190/discussions/0/2727382174632621532/ so it may not be a proton specific issue.  The workaround described in that post worked for me.  After all that, the game ran smoothly enough. It did crash a few times throughout my 33 hours of play, but since the game auto-saves, it wasn't too painful.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes:
I got the game to laungch now. I had to delete the render.cfg file for the game to launch. This brought up a few new problems though:

-You need to set the graphics settings every time you lauch the game.
-After closing the game with new graphics settings, you can no longer lauch the game until you delete render.cfg again.
-If there is no render.cfg, the game will launch with the default low resolution. The resolution can't be changed in-game, because it doesn't recognize the screen resolution. And as far as i know, the resolution can only be set manually by changing it in render.cfg.

I also tried making a rended.cfg file with only "ScreenHeight16by9" and "ScreenWidth16by9". The game launched, but the resolution seemed unaffected.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: Unable to start with error message "Installed video device does not support DirectX 11 features."
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: Game won't start. After clicking "play" displays "running" for a few seconds and then quits.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: As mentioned in other reports as well, I needed to manually edit render.cfg with '"ScreenHeight16by9" "1080.000000"' and '"ScreenWidth16by9" "1920.000000"' to fit my screen resolution, as it is not properly detected in-game. The game is then somewhat playable, but is known to crash sporadically when progressing through the game from gameplay to cut-scenes, and/or back again. This makes is especially tricky when a mission has numerous of such switches back and forth and crashes just before you could complete the mission.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: Only thing I had to do was to set resolution in the cfg file as it would not detect my monitor. However I did not run it in a Window I ran full screen. I don't have Windows installed for a Platform comparison but I have a GTX 1070 and on high settings I get 30 FPS on the benchmark but appears to play well when playing. (Was comfortable and no issues when playing) I played for over two hours. Multiple cut scenes and gameplay cuts and no crashes. Game seems stable.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: Launches fine, auto-detects settings and resolution (or just edit render.cfg to set desired resolution). Running below spec on low settings benchmarks an ugly but just about playable 24fps (little/no drops). PS4 and Steam Controllers work apart from some issues where button prompts/hints are missing. Mouse and touchpad are detected on startup but not in-game, so no action or camera control (not linux/proton specific issue). Couldn't resolve this but some Windows users worked around by disabling Logitech Gaming Software: https://steamcommunity.com/app/356190/discussions/0/1698294337783003244/
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes:
Had to manually edit the file "render.cfg in /steamapps/compatdata/356190/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local Settings/Application Data/WBGames/Shadow of War for my favored resolution.

 I play the game in 1600 x 900 on high settings with average 60fps.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: Didn't start with Proton 3.16-4 but started and ran very well with Proton 3.16-7 Beta
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes:
Manually edit ".steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/356190/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local\ Settings/Application\ Data/WB\ Games/Shadow\ of\ War/render.cfg" for Resolution
(for me 1920x1080 - '"ScreenHeight16by9" "1080.000000"' and '"ScreenWidth16by9" "1920.000000"' )
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: No issues, people talk about fixing resolution, but mine was fine to begin with.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes:
Works pretty well, no graphical or audio glitches and a smooth 60fps on high settings. Had to modify the `steamapps/compatdata/356190/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local Settings/Application\ Data/WB\ Games/Shadow of War/render.cfg` to set the correct resolution though. That seems to happen on Windows too, so I'm not too concerned about it.

Still haven't completed the game, but after making it to the third area I don't think there's anything that would make this a bad experience, definitely recommended :)
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: As others have mentioned, this game works perfectly after a minor tweak: Set your screen resolution manually by editing "steamapps/compatdata/356190/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local Settings/Application Data/WB Games/Shadow of War/render.cfg"
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: Just had to adjust the resolution file and then it worked like a charm.

notes: need to manually configure resolution to 1080p, other than that smooth 60 fps with v sync on.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes:
Set your screen resolution manually by editing "steamapps/compatdata/356190/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local Settings/Application Data/WB Games/Shadow of War/render.cfg"

Set Proton version 3.16-6 and play.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: All I had to do was change the resolution manually as others have said (go to "steamapps/compatdata/356190/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local Settings/Application Data/WB Games/Shadow of War/render.cfg" and edit ScreenHeight16by9 and ScreenWidth16by9 to the correct resolution). I was able to run at low and some mixed settings at 1440p, at around 70fps, with my setup (RX580, ryzen 1600 and 16GB ram).
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: Had to change the resolution manually in the render.cfg file. It ran somewhat well at around medium settings, but was only getting 30-40 fps.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: go to "steamapps/compatdata/356190/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local Settings/Application Data/WB Games/Shadow of War/render.cfg" and edit ScreenHeight16by9 and ScreenWidth16by9 to the resolution you want - just like the person before me said. Works perfectly on 2k. If the game has stutters, they also exist in the Windows version.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes:
After the tweaks mentioned by others (changing resolution in config) I managed to get the game running with correct graphics etc. However, upon reaching an early on Main Quest the game simply freezes up. I tried to mess around with a few settings/restart etc however eventually lost my patience. 

Maybe you can get it running with more tweaks/work-around but overall I feel the crashes take away from the experience and it's forced me to boot the game up on my windows instead. Wouldn't recommend a play-through on Linux at the moment unless you are truly desperate and have no possibilities of playing it on Windows.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes:
60fps and relatively smooth gameplay on proton 4.2.4 at 1920x1080 on ultra. 
Some custscenes soft-lock the game and require a kill of the 'MainThread' process. Unsure what's causing it since restarting steam will often fix the issue. Can be annoying when there's multiple cutscenes back to back.
Tweaks mentioned in other reports required if you want to set your own resolution and not run at the abysmal default resolution.

notes:

notes: runs perfect but only wants to run at the 960x540 resolution. i get average of 125 fps.  cant change the resolution.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: Should be noted that the resolution issue is an issue related to the game and affects users in Windows as well as Linux (does not depend if you are using Proton or not). So aside from having to configure the render.cfg file, the game works perfectly in full resolution and max settings.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes:
Just make sure that you have latest Porton version and Nvidia drivers. For the resolution change you need to edit render.cfg file: (...)/steamapps/compatdata/356190/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local Settings/Application Data/WB Games/Shadow of War/

P.S. No need for using- " Set launch options ".
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes:
The game won't detect your correct native resolution and you have to manually edit the render.cfg in "steamapps/compatdata/356190/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local\ Settings/Application\ Data/WB Games". Specifically ScreenHeight16by9 and ScreenWidth16by9. After this the game runs fine apart from one issue.

The game often freezes completely after moving from a cut-scene to gameplay, or vice versa. You'll have to manually kill the process and restart the game. This often makes you lose your progress in various mission which makes the game quite annoying to play. I have also experienced random crash to desktops during play, although they are quite rare.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes:
The resolution must be set in SteamLibrary/steamapps/compatdata/356190/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local Settings/Application Data/WB Games/Shadow of War/ otherwise the game renders at a lower res, although this issue doesn't seem to affect everyone.

On proton 4.11-2, the game had recurring freezes, some of them requiring to kill the process. Using proton 3.16-9 eliminated them.

I've been playing 5+ hours with no issues now and flawless performance compared to Windows.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: Had to edit the render.cfg file to get my resolution right. No problems running the game on high graphics setting, about 60fps. Occasional lag or stutter, and I've had the game crash and lock-up on me a couple times in about 20 hours of playtime. Otherwise the game runs fine, very playable.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: steam launch parameter PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% to stop stutter in cutscenes, resolution has to be set manually in files,and changing settings causes framerate to freeze at either 8 or 20 fps,however restarting or changing any settings again fixes the issue,game runs perfectly after said tweaks as long as you dont play with any settings(graphical or controls) to avoid said issue
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: But sometimes starting does not work, than a restart of steam helps.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes:
Had to edit the render.cfg to change the ingame resolution.

Disabling Esync seems to have prevented crashing after/before cinematics completely.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes:
So firstly the resolution is an issue apparently on proton and native windows PC's..

Fix to this is to change them manually in steamapps/compatdata/356190/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local Settings/Application Data/WB Games/Shadow of War/render.cfg (under ScreenHeight16by9 and ScreenWidth16by9).

However you NEED TO TURN OFF DYNAMIC RESOLUTION SCALING in the options menu as the game will detect your PC as severely lacking with buffer overflow values etc through proton and think it is running slow the entire time setting it to the minimum resolution.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: Edit the config file to change the resolution. Runs great other than that. Doesn't run with Esync.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™
